page: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro System hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs using the x86_64 architecture, driven by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It lacks local storage and dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage and featuring a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the m7a.16xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU tests. Its memory subsystem achieves top-tier read-only bandwidth for small cached blocks, alongside strong performance in large-block read and write operations. The processor's instruction set, which includes AVX-512 extensions, supports top-tier software compilation speeds and strong database operations. For LLM inference workloads, the instance achieves top-tier throughput for small model prompt processing and medium model text generation, though text generation for smaller models and prompt processing for larger models fall into average tiers.

From a resource density perspective, the instance offers high compute density with its dedicated physical cores and high-speed DDR5 memory, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded workloads that benefit from high memory bandwidth. However, the absence of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network storage, which may introduce I/O latency tradeoffs for storage-heavy applications. The m7a.16xlarge is highly suited for enterprise applications, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores and 64 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro System, it includes 256.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core, with a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmarks, the m7a.16xlarge delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, alongside strong database and software compilation capabilities. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density compute tasks, though the lack of local storage requires network-attached alternatives. This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 256 GB DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m7a.16xlarge features 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 256 GB DDR5 RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high-speed DDR5 memory for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
256 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory (4 GB per core)
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmark performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with Nitro hypervisor integration
Best suited for database operations, software compilation, and CPU-based LLM inference
Tradeoff of no local storage, requiring network-attached storage solutions
